name: config_multireads
description: Force repeated reads of config.py across 5 turns to exercise native Read cache interception
task_prompt:
TURN 1 OF 5

Read archolith_proxy/config.py in full. Count exactly how many fields are defined in the Settings class (not properties, not validators — only class-level fields with type annotations). Output just the count and stop.

follow_up_turns.message:
TURN 2 OF 5

Re-read archolith_proxy/config.py from scratch. List only the boolean fields (type `bool`). Output each field name and its default value. Do not use your memory of earlier turns — read the file again now.

TURN 3 OF 5

Re-read archolith_proxy/config.py from scratch. List only the integer fields (type `int`). Output each field name and its default value. Do not use your memory of earlier turns — read the file again now.

TURN 4 OF 5

Re-read archolith_proxy/config.py from scratch. List only the string fields (type `str`). Output each field name and its default value. Do not use your memory of earlier turns — read the file again now.

TURN 5 OF 5

Re-read archolith_proxy/config.py one final time. Create scripts/config_audit.py that prints every field name in the Settings class, one per line, with its type and default. The script must import nothing from archolith_proxy — use hardcoded field data from your read. Do not modify any existing file.

checkpoints.description: config_audit.py script exists and references Settings fields
